PROMPT: I'm planning a road trip to visit two famous national parks and want to compare their accessibility and current conditions. First, search for national parks in Utah (state code 'UT') and find the park whose name contains 'Zion' — note its park code. Then get the full details for that park and note its exact GPS entrance coordinates (latitude and longitude). Next, search for national parks in Arizona (state code 'AZ') and find the park whose name contains 'Grand Canyon' — note its park code. Get the full details for that Grand Canyon park and note its GPS entrance coordinates. Now use the GPS coordinates of the Zion park entrance as the origin and the GPS coordinates of the Grand Canyon entrance as the destination to get driving directions between them. Finally, check for any current alerts at BOTH parks (using their park codes) and report: (1) the driving distance and estimated travel time between the two park entrances, (2) how many total active alerts exist across both parks combined, and (3) the title of the first alert listed for each park (or 'No alerts' if none).
